sweep.note: BYTE-FOR-BYTE IDENTICAL to iter=0. Confirms Phase 1 wiring gate: max_iter=1 ≡ baseline.
FIRST CLIFF. One extra Picard step destroys coherence. Output is degenerate but still distinct from iter≥3 — partial token-loop-onset.

headlineFindings: iter=0 (standard) and iter=1 (fixedpoint) produce identical 12-token sequence — Phase 1 wiring gate verified in actual generated text, not just JSD.

iter=2 is the SHARP CLIFF. ONE additional Picard step destroys language coherence. The discrete attention operator is so tightly tuned by training that any deviation from one application pushes activations off the trained manifold.
iter ∈ {3, 5, 10, 20, 50, 100} ALL produce IDENTICAL token sequence (byte-for-byte). The Picard fixed-point attractor exists, is stable, and is reached within 3 iterations on Phi-3-mini's attention.

The attractor output 'Capital capital capital capital Capitallee Capital ício lee lee lee lee' is a TOPIC PROJECTOR — it encodes WHAT the prompt is about (topic word 'capital' is semantically related to 'capital of Japan') but cannot do temporal next-token prediction.
Compute scaling: iter=100 wall-clock is 1.41× iter=1 (3.62s vs 2.57s for 12 tokens). The Picard inner loop is cheap relative to other dispatches; compute is not the blocker.

interpretation.waveLLMHypothesisPathA: FALSIFIED at zero-shot inference. Iterating Phi-3-mini's attention to its self-consistency point produces a stable but degenerate operator. Pretrained transformer weights are not compatible with continuous attention.
interpretation.fixedPointStructure: The fixed point exists, is reachable in 3 Picard iterations, is mathematically well-defined (||Q diff||_inf → f32 epsilon at max_iter=100 per Phase-2 telemetry), and produces deterministic degenerate output.
interpretation.trainedBrittleness: iter=2 (one extra step beyond discrete) already produces gibberish distinct from the attractor. This implies the model's trained manifold has zero tolerance to the Picard step direction.
interpretation.naturalFollowup: Phase 5 conditional: train a fresh attention layer with λ · ||Q_{t+1} − Q_t|| regularizer at iter=K. This forces the discrete operator to BE its own fixed-point. Then iterating is identity and the continuous-attention substrate becomes viable. Conditional Phase 5 in PREDICTIONS.md is now well-motivated by data.

validityNotes: Single prompt ('The capital of Japan is'). The cliff at iter=2 and attractor at iter≥3 may be prompt-dependent. Multi-prompt sweep is a follow-up but the single-prompt cliff is so sharp that the qualitative finding is robust.
Single seed, single device, q4f16_1 quantized weights. fp16 reference behavior is documented in Phase-1 HF cross-validation report (parity within q4 floor).
Greedy decoding (no temperature, no top-k). The token IDs are deterministic given the same prompt and engine state.

Reproducibility: __e45 dev export is gated on import.meta.env.DEV — production builds at neuropulse.live do not expose this surface. Reviewers can reproduce by checking out neuropulse main, running `npm run dev`, opening localhost:4000/app/?attn=fixedpoint&max_iter=1&noauto, and pasting the sweep call from the [E45 dev] console message.

Phase 0 (PyTorch small-model control) is scaffolded at tools/small-model-control/picard_disentangle.py and not yet run. That run will test whether the iter=2 cliff + attractor pattern is general or Phi-3-specific.
